steps.title: Define the task requirement
steps.content: The student must connect Browner's quotation to a specific composition. That means the answer must (1) include or reference the Browner quote and (2) apply it directly to an identifiable piece — not to Ballard's general body of work.

steps.title: Eliminate and match
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Consistent with Browner's observation that Ballard's music "relies on Indigenous instruments, rhythms, forms,...and other musical elements," *Cacéga Ayuwípi* incorporates a Navajo (Diné) water drum.': Opens with the Browner quotation and immediately applies it to Cacéga Ayuwípi by citing the Navajo water drum as a concrete example. Both requirements met. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Browner notes that Ballard's music "relies on Indigenous instruments, rhythms, forms,...and other musical elements," further indicating that it synthesizes Western classical music with elements of various Native music traditions.': Uses the quotation but only speaks to Ballard's general style — no specific composition named. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Discussing Ballard's body of works, Browner observes that it relies on elements of various Native musical traditions.': Paraphrases Browner's general observation and again refers only to his overall body of work, not a specific piece. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Browner's writing discusses Ballard, the classical music composer responsible for *Cacéga Ayuwípi*.': Names the composition but does not incorporate or connect the Browner quotation to it. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
